author: terencechain
content: <@&1417820113981145228> quick one for debugging Prysm going offline: for execution_payload_envelopes_by_range, can your response ever be non-contiguous, i.e. two consecutive envelopes where the later one's parent_hash doesn't equal the previous one's block_hash ?

author: potuz
content: Disclaimer: already drunk a Saturday in the pre for the game and after seeing Colombia being outright robbed by some fat finger justifying it with a computer. Anyway even in these conditions I think the above should never happen

author: terencechain
content: we are getting `envelope parent hash does not match previous hash` in the response, so it could also be a prysm bug, but that's why we are having trouble syncing (again), that error also downscores peer so we now have no peer

author: potuz
content: yeah, I've seen this before, not sure if it's a Prysm bug or the serving client. Anyway good spot to plug in yet-again an "I told you so" and "we should have merged EIP 8237"

author: terencechain
content:
i understand the pattern more now
- we cant reconstruct the payload from blind to full, for some reasons ELs gave us nil
- we respond block by range, but payload by range we would just respond empty
- prysm bans prysm here because if we get a empty by range, we would assume all the block is empty, but that's not the case! (im not sure other clients downscore for this or just prysm)

still looking into why payload reconstruction is doomed

author: potuz
content: this would explain Prysm banning Prysm, but not Prysm banning others, so we should be able to sync otherwise

author: potuz
content: either way, from the description it looks like a Prysm bug: if we fail to reconstruct a payload, we shouldn't serve the range at all instead of serving empty

author: terencechain
content:
im not sure if other clients downscore as well. Something to confirm with everyone
the spec allows payload gaps so its not a mandatory downscore so others may implement that if payload response is empty, i will try my next peer. That gets complicated though

author: potuz
content: what the fuck? how can we allow for gaps in by-range requests?

author: potuz
content: I give up on the APIs,

author: terencechain
content: Empty. One reason could be i lost my ability to reconstruct payload but i still have the consensus blocks. Surely you could argue at this point id not respond consensus block at all

author: terencechain
content: Ah i know what the prysm bug is. I'm using head state (because it's cheap) to verify prev randao for bid. This breaks during forking.
